applicabilityOps.semantics: Logical AND / conjunction. Applicable iff EVERY child operand evaluates true. Deterministic, no arbitrary code (DOC-007 §18 forbids JS/SQL). Boolean node composing leaves into a tree. DOC-007 §18 lines 1240-1243.
Logical OR / disjunction. Applicable iff AT LEAST ONE child operand evaluates true. DOC-007 §18 lines 1244-1247.

applicabilityOps.operands: operand: ApplicabilityExpression (SINGULAR key `operand`, exactly one child — NOT `operands`)
applicabilityOps.semantics: Logical negation. True iff its single child evaluates false. IMPLEMENTATION GOTCHA: NOT uses singular `operand` while ALL/ANY use plural `operands`; wrong key = silent evaluator bug. DOC-007 §18 lines 1248-1251.

applicabilityOps.operands: path: string (JSONPath-like, e.g. $.objectType, $.pwuKind); value: string | number | boolean
applicabilityOps.semantics: Scalar strict equality. Resolve `path` against the subject ObjectEnvelope and test strict equality to `value`. DOC-007 §18 lines 1252-1256.

applicabilityOps.operands: path: string; values: Array<string | number> (plural `values` list)
applicabilityOps.semantics: Set membership. True iff the scalar at `path` is a member of `values` (e.g. $.workLifecycleState IN [EVIDENCE_PENDING, UNDER_ASSURANCE]). DOC-007 §18 lines 1257-1261.

applicabilityOps.operands: path: string; value: string (SINGULAR `value`, unlike IN's `values`)
applicabilityOps.semantics: Containment. True iff the collection/string at `path` contains `value` (array-membership or substring). DOC-007 §18 lines 1262-1266.
Presence test. True iff a value is present at `path` on the subject (existence, independent of truthiness). DOC-007 §18 lines 1267-1270.

applicabilityOps.operands: dimension: CONSEQUENCE | UNCERTAINTY | IRREVERSIBILITY | SECURITY_SENSITIVITY | REGULATORY_EXPOSURE; level: LOW | MEDIUM | HIGH | CRITICAL
applicabilityOps.semantics: Ordinal risk threshold. True iff the subject's risk on `dimension` >= `level` over the ordered scale LOW < MEDIUM < HIGH < CRITICAL. This is the hook that escalates the required INDEPENDENCE tier and drives the REQUIRED/RECOMMENDED/OPTIONAL applicability outcome. DOC-007 §18 lines 1271-1284.

admissibilityConditions.condition: It is relevant to the assessed claim (evidence is not proof by existence — §6.3)

dispositionRules.rule: AUTHORITY SEPARATION: validators only RECOMMEND. ValidatorResult.dispositionRecommendation ∈ {SATISFIED, CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED, REJECTED, INCONCLUSIVE, ESCALATED} — exactly 5 values. WAIVED is NOT a validator recommendation and is NOT a DispositionRule target; it is set only by an authorized actor via the Assurance Service. The validator recommendation does not itself mutate assurance state (Invariant 10).

CRITERION-RESULT OUTCOMES: every criterion result MUST be exactly one of MET | PARTIALLY_MET | NOT_MET | NOT_APPLICABLE | UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE (uppercase enum per §33/§20.1). UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE is a first-class distinct outcome and CANNOT be treated as MET (not a silent pass).

DISPOSITION MEANINGS: SATISFIED = admissible evidence supports the claims under criteria, subject to recorded limitations; CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED = supportable only if explicit conditions remain true / required follow-up occurs; REJECTED = one or more material claims unsupported/contradicted OR a blocking criterion violated; INCONCLUSIVE = evidence insufficient to support OR reject; ESCALATED = validator/policy cannot resolve within its authority/competence; WAIVED = authorized actor accepts proceeding without satisfaction (governance-only).

DEFAULT SINGLE-POLICY PRECEDENCE LADDER (override-able by policy): CRITICAL open finding → REJECTED or ESCALATED; BLOCKING open finding → REJECTED; MATERIAL open finding → CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED / INCONCLUSIVE / REJECTED; evidence deficit → INCONCLUSIVE; all mandatory criteria met → SATISFIED.

STRICTEST-UNRESOLVED AGGREGATE (multi-policy) — NEVER A NUMERIC AVERAGE. The aggregate must preserve the strictest unresolved disposition relevant to the work and MUST NOT be reduced to a numerical average and MUST NOT silently average disagreement. Resolution order: any critical rejection → REJECTED; any blocking rejection → REJECTED; any required assessment missing → EVIDENCE_REQUIRED or UNASSESSED; any inconclusive required assessment → INCONCLUSIVE; any conditional required assessment → CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED; all required assessments satisfied → SATISFIED. A SATISFIED advisory policy NEVER overrides a REJECTED blocking policy; policy conflict is surfaced and results remain independently inspectable.

CONFLICTING VALIDATORS: conflicting assessments remain visible; the system MAY invoke a tie-breaking validator, gather discriminating evidence, escalate, or permit an authorized human decision — but MUST NOT silently average disagreement. Aggregate becomes 'contested'.

ASSURANCE SERVICE OVERRIDES THE RECOMMENDATION: if a validator recommends SATISFIED while a mandatory criterion is NOT_MET, the Assurance Service rejects the recommendation at the boundary (unsupported pass is not accepted; every required criterion must have a result — Invariant 5).
dispositionRules.sourceSection: DOC-004 Conformance Test 3 (lines 2091-2095); DOC-007 §20.2 (disposition contradicts mandatory policy rules)

dispositionRules.rule: EXECUTION != ASSURANCE: ExecutionStepSucceeded cannot produce AssuranceState=SATISFIED; a baseline cannot be promoted solely because all execution steps completed; assurance stays UNASSESSED until required policies complete. Open CRITICAL findings block satisfaction; open BLOCKING findings block baseline promotion.
dispositionRules.sourceSection: DOC-004 Invariants 11,12,20, Test 9, §10.3, §26.6; DOC-007 §16 last line, §35.2

dispositionRules.rule: CONFIDENCE IS NOT EVIDENCE / EVIDENCE IS NOT PROOF BY EXISTENCE: a SATISFIED claim MUST reference admissible evidence; unscoped confidence cannot substitute; a test result / execution trace / citation / validator opinion does not by itself prove coverage/intent/correctness/independence unless the relationship is shown or policy permits professional judgment.

DispositionRule SHAPE: { disposition: SATISFIED|CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED|REJECTED|INCONCLUSIVE|ESCALATED; condition: PolicyExpression; requiredEvidenceIds?: string[]; forbiddenOpenSeverities?: string[]; requiredIndependence?: IndependenceRequirement }. WAIVED is not a DispositionRule target — waiver is a separate governed act.

SEVERITY SCALE feeding precedence (findings/criteria): INFORMATIONAL | ADVISORY | MATERIAL | BLOCKING | CRITICAL (AssessmentCriterion.severityIfNotMet §7; FindingDefinition.defaultSeverity §9.1; AssuranceObservation.severity DOC-007 §21).

EVIDENCE-ACCESS FAILURE (distinct from validator failure and from rejection): if required evidence cannot be retrieved, disposition becomes EVIDENCE_PENDING or INCONCLUSIVE — do NOT infer evidence content. Invalidated evidence triggers reassessment of dependent claims; a semantic change to the subject drives SATISFIED/CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED → INVALIDATED.

independenceTiers.tier: IndependenceRequirement enum ladder (ascending strength): NONE → DIFFERENT_INVOCATION → DIFFERENT_CONTEXT_INSTANCE → DIFFERENT_AGENT → DIFFERENT_MODEL → DIFFERENT_PROVIDER → HUMAN → ORGANIZATIONALLY_INDEPENDENT
independenceTiers.requires: The declared level, VERIFIED (multi-dimensional) by comparing producer vs evaluator invocation, agent identity, model identity, provider, shared hidden context, shared prompt lineage, and organizational authority. A validator is NOT independent merely because it is labeled a 'Verifier'.

DIFFERENT_INVOCATION (same model permitted in a separate invocation). Advisory findings; limited evidence package; no human approval unless a material change emerges; policy-based auto-acceptance allowed ONLY for low-risk reversible work.

DIFFERENT_AGENT role from the producer; a DIFFERENT_MODEL is PREFERRED for MATERIAL claims; independent evidence review; blocking findings enabled; human approval for material scope, architecture, or baseline changes.

DIFFERENT_MODEL or DIFFERENT_PROVIDER, PLUS HUMAN or ORGANIZATIONALLY_INDEPENDENT assessment for CRITICAL claims; stronger evidence; explicit residual risk; restricted waivers; immutable assessment package.

independenceTiers.tier: RISK → INDEPENDENCE MAPPING (derived; keyed on claim materiality/risk assessed via RISK_AT_LEAST over CONSEQUENCE/UNCERTAINTY/IRREVERSIBILITY/SECURITY_SENSITIVITY/REGULATORY_EXPOSURE)
independenceTiers.requires: low/reversible → DIFFERENT_INVOCATION (Lightweight); material → DIFFERENT_AGENT + DIFFERENT_MODEL preferred (Standard); critical → DIFFERENT_MODEL or DIFFERENT_PROVIDER + HUMAN or ORGANIZATIONALLY_INDEPENDENT (High assurance). The RISK_AT_LEAST applicability op is the trigger that escalates the required tier.
independenceTiers.riskLevel: Mapping spans Lightweight → Standard → High assurance (DOC-004 §8.3, §36; Semantic Inventory Decision 4)

independenceTiers.requires: INDEPENDENCE GATES SATISFACTION: if required independence is not satisfied the assessment CANNOT receive SATISFIED; an Independence Violation Observation is created; another evaluator must be invoked or a waiver obtained. State: ASSESSING → INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION; error RPH_VALIDATOR_INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION.
independenceTiers.riskLevel: Gate on all tiers (DOC-004 §8.4 lines 458-462, §30; DOC-007 §25.1)

validatorContractFields.type: enum: MODEL | DETERMINISTIC | HYBRID | HUMAN | EXTERNAL_SERVICE
SchemaReference (boundary-rejection uses this to schema-validate ValidatorResult; failure → RPH_VALIDATOR_OUTPUT_INVALID)
object { timeoutSeconds?: number; maximumRetries?: number; maximumEvidenceItems?: number; maximumContextTokens?: number } — object present, each sub-field optional

validatorFailedConditions: VALIDATOR_FAILED (DOC-004 §34.1; state ASSESSING → VALIDATOR_FAILED, §30) = validator EXECUTION failure: the validator could NOT complete / produce a usable result (crash, timeout, exceeded maximumRetries, tool/model/provider error, unavailable). Produces VALIDATOR_FAILED + execution error + incomplete assessment + a retry / alternate-validator / escalation action. It is NEVER an assurance REJECTED disposition (Core Invariant 9, line 2051).

BOUNDARY-REJECTION (DOC-007 §20.2; DOC-004 §34.2, Invariant 10) is DISTINCT from VALIDATOR_FAILED: a result WAS returned but the Assurance Service rejects it at the boundary with a typed RphError + retry/alternate/escalate. It does NOT become a REJECTED disposition and does NOT create authoritative findings from unparsed prose. The Assurance Service MUST reject a validator result when:
BOUNDARY-REJECTION [1] policy identity or version mismatches (RPH_POLICY_VERSION_MISMATCH). DOC-007 §20.2.
BOUNDARY-REJECTION [3] subject semantic version mismatches (RPH_SUBJECT_VERSION_MISMATCH). DOC-007 §20.2, §35.1.
BOUNDARY-REJECTION [4] required criteria are missing. DOC-007 §20.2.
BOUNDARY-REJECTION [5] referenced evidence does not exist (RPH_EVIDENCE_MISSING). DOC-007 §20.2.
BOUNDARY-REJECTION [7] disposition contradicts mandatory policy rules (e.g. recommends SATISFIED while a mandatory criterion is NOT_MET). DOC-007 §20.2; DOC-004 Test 3.

BOUNDARY-REJECTION [8] output fails schema validation (RPH_VALIDATOR_OUTPUT_INVALID) — malformed/incomplete output rejected at the boundary; preserve raw output for diagnostics; record schema-validation failure; create NO authoritative findings from unparsed prose; retry or use another validator. DOC-007 §20.2; DOC-004 §34.2.
BOUNDARY-REJECTION [9] independence requirements are not satisfied (RPH_VALIDATOR_INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION; state ASSESSING → INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION). DOC-007 §20.2; DOC-004 §8.4.

EVIDENCE-ACCESS FAILURE is a related-but-separate boundary outcome: if required evidence cannot be retrieved, disposition becomes EVIDENCE_PENDING or INCONCLUSIVE (do NOT infer evidence content) — it is NOT VALIDATOR_FAILED and NOT REJECTED. DOC-004 §34.3.

KEY DISTINCTION (what makes it VALIDATOR_FAILED rather than a rejection): a REJECTED disposition requires a WELL-FORMED, schema-valid result whose one-or-more MATERIAL claims are unsupported/contradicted or which violates a BLOCKING criterion. If the validator did not run OR produced malformed/incomplete/unparseable output, the outcome is VALIDATOR_FAILED (execution failure) or a §20.2 boundary RphError (bad result) → retry / alternate validator / escalate — NEVER an authoritative rejection. NEITHER may mutate authoritative state (Invariants 9 & 10, lines 2051-2053).
waiverRules: WaiverRule SHAPE (DOC-004 §12.1, lines 622-636): { waiverAllowed: boolean; eligibleCriteriaIds: string[]; prohibitedFindingSeverities: string[]; requiredAuthorityType: string; maximumDuration?: string; requiredRationaleFields: string[]; requiredCompensatingControls?: string[]; revalidationTrigger?: PolicyExpression }.

A WAIVER MUST RECORD (DOC-004 §12.2, lines 641-651): exact policy and criterion; exact object AND semantic version; the finding being waived; authority; rationale; duration/expiration; compensating controls; downstream impact; review conditions.

A WAIVER MUST NOT (DOC-004 §12.2, Invariant 13, lines 653-658): erase a finding; transform false evidence into valid evidence; declare a rejected claim true; apply to future semantic versions unless explicitly renewed.

SCOPE BINDING (Invariant 14, line 2061; §28.1): a waiver applies ONLY to its specified policy + criterion + object. A waiver on one policy/criterion does NOT cover another; prohibitedFindingSeverities blocks waiving findings at/above the listed severities.

VERSION BINDING (Invariant 14; Conformance Test 7, lines 2115-2119): a waiver bound to object semantic version N does NOT apply to version N+1 (a waiver for architecture v2 does NOT apply to v3); requires explicit renewal.

AUTHORITY CEILING (§26.7 line 1512, §15.11 lines 824-826): critical integrity failures cannot be waived by ordinary product authority; unauthorized intent alteration cannot be waived AT ALL — an authorized user-approved revised intent yields a governed intent revision, NOT a waiver.

LIFECYCLE / GOVERNANCE-ONLY: WAIVED is a disposition set ONLY via governed commands (requestAssuranceWaiver / grantAssuranceWaiver / denyAssuranceWaiver / expireAssuranceWaiver, §32); it is NOT a validator recommendation and NOT a DispositionRule target. State WAIVED → WAIVER_EXPIRED on expiry (§30). Waivers must be included in the baseline-promotion package (§26.4); an expired/invalid waiver is itself a baseline-promotion finding INVALID_OR_EXPIRED_WAIVER (§26.5).

conflicts.topic: ValidatorResult schema divergence: DOC-007 §20 includes `subjectSemanticVersions: Record<string,number>` (load-bearing for the §20.2 subject-version-mismatch boundary rejection and §35.1 version binding), whereas DOC-004 §4.2 ValidatorResult omits it.
conflicts.resolution: Prefer the DOC-007 §20 form. The Contract Package is the serialization authority (README precedence: domain/contracts outrank convenience descriptions). Verified present at DOC-007 lines 1427. Keep subjectSemanticVersions REQUIRED.

conflicts.topic: WAIVED appears in the disposition-MEANINGS list (DOC-004 §10.1) but is NOT in the validator dispositionRecommendation set (§4.2 / DOC-007 §20 = 5 values) nor a DispositionRule target (§10.2).
conflicts.resolution: Reconcile as: WAIVED is a governance-only assurance-state outcome, never a validator recommendation. The dispositionRecommendation enum is exactly {SATISFIED, CONDITIONALLY_SATISFIED, REJECTED, INCONCLUSIVE, ESCALATED}; the assessment-state SUPERSET additionally includes WAIVED, VALIDATOR_FAILED, INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION, INVALIDATED, CANCELLED.

conflicts.topic: Aggregate resolution (§28.2) introduces states EVIDENCE_REQUIRED and UNASSESSED that are NOT members of the per-assessment disposition enum (§10.1).
conflicts.resolution: Treat the aggregate/cross-policy vocabulary as a superset of the single-assessment disposition enum: EVIDENCE_REQUIRED / UNASSESSED are aggregate-level states meaning 'a required assessment is missing', not per-assessment dispositions. Preserve them at aggregate granularity; do not coerce them into INCONCLUSIVE (strictest-unresolved must be preserved, never averaged).

conflicts.topic: ApplicabilityExpression DSL op set (ALL/ANY/NOT/EQUALS/IN/CONTAINS/EXISTS/RISK_AT_LEAST) is NOT defined in DOC-004; that file references the DSL only opaquely as `PolicyExpression` (§5.1 line 277, §10.2 line 552, §12.1 line 635, §13 line 666). The concrete grammar lives in DOC-007 §18.
conflicts.resolution: Treat `PolicyExpression` (DOC-004) and `ApplicabilityExpression` (DOC-007 §18) as the SAME declarative code-free DSL. DOC-004 §5.1 ApplicabilityRule is a higher-level wrapper whose `expression` slot carries the §18 DSL; applicability yields an ApplicabilityOutcome (§5.2: REQUIRED | RECOMMENDED | OPTIONAL | NOT_APPLICABLE | REQUIRES_HUMAN_DETERMINATION).

conflicts.topic: DSL key-name asymmetry (silent-bug hazard): NOT uses singular `operand` while ALL/ANY use plural `operands`; CONTAINS/EQUALS use singular `value` while IN uses plural `values`.
conflicts.resolution: Verified against DOC-007 §18 source (lines 1240-1266). Bind evaluator/serializer to these exact keys: ALL.operands[], ANY.operands[], NOT.operand, EQUALS.value, IN.values[], CONTAINS.value. This is an implementation gotcha, not a semantic conflict.

conflicts.topic: VALIDATOR_FAILED (execution failure, §34.1) vs BOUNDARY-REJECTION (§20.2 / §34.2) are two DISTINCT non-disposition mechanisms that must not be collapsed.
conflicts.resolution: Preserve both as separate outcomes: VALIDATOR_FAILED = no usable result (assessment state VALIDATOR_FAILED); BOUNDARY-REJECTION = a result returned but rejected at §20.2 (typed RphError). Neither may become a REJECTED assurance disposition; neither may mutate authoritative state (Invariants 9 & 10).

openItems.item: JSONPath-like `path` resolution semantics for leaf ops (EQUALS/IN/CONTAINS/EXISTS) are specified only as 'resolved deterministically against the subject object envelope' with examples ($.objectType, $.pwuKind, $.workLifecycleState). The exact supported JSONPath subset (nesting, arrays, wildcards, missing-path behavior) is not defined.
openItems.why: Determinism (DOC-007 §18: no arbitrary JS/SQL) requires a pinned, spec'd resolver grammar; ambiguity risks per-implementation divergence in applicability outcomes.

openItems.item: CONTAINS is defined as 'the collection/string at path contains value' but does not disambiguate array-membership vs substring behavior when `path` resolves to different types.
openItems.why: Type-dependent evaluation of the same node could yield different applicability results across subjects; needs a typed-behavior rule.

openItems.item: RISK_AT_LEAST reads the subject's risk on a dimension, but how the per-dimension risk profile (CONSEQUENCE/UNCERTAINTY/IRREVERSIBILITY/SECURITY_SENSITIVITY/REGULATORY_EXPOSURE) is populated/sourced/provenanced on the subject envelope is not specified in DOC-004/DOC-007 §18.
openItems.why: The risk→independence escalation and REQUIRED/RECOMMENDED gating hinge on these values; unsourced risk = ungoverned tier selection.

openItems.item: CriterionResult schema (DOC-007 §20.1) has no claim-level status, but the DOC-004 §33 example nests criterionResults under each claimResult and adds a claim-level `recommendedStatus` (e.g. CONTESTED) not present in the §20 ValidatorResult/ClaimAssessmentResult contract.
openItems.why: A field used in the canonical example but absent from the authoritative schema is a contract gap; 'CONTESTED' also overlaps the §34.4 aggregate 'contested' concept and needs a single definition.

openItems.item: Aggregate 'contested' outcome (§34.4, Test 8) and the tie-break process (invoke tie-breaking validator / gather discriminating evidence / escalate / authorized human decision) are described but the selection criteria and who has authority to pick the path are not fully specified.
openItems.why: Without a deterministic trigger and authority binding, 'must not silently average' is stated but the resolution routing is under-defined.

openItems.item: DispositionRule.requiredIndependence (§10.2) vs ValidatorContract.producerIndependenceMetadata (§4.1) vs §8.2 verification: the docs state independence must be verified multi-dimensionally but do not pin WHICH component computes the comparison or how DIFFERENT_CONTEXT_INSTANCE (shared hidden context / shared prompt lineage) is detected in practice.
openItems.why: The independence GATE (§8.4) blocks SATISFIED; an unspecified detector for shared-context/prompt-lineage leaves a verifiable-in-principle but unimplementable rule.
